Iron County Medical Care Facility

Address
1523 U.S. Highway 2, Crystal Falls, MI 49920

Setting
Rural
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.20 3.23 5.02 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.69 0.56 0.82 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.70 0.72 0.84 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.81 1.95 3.36 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.39 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.38
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.38
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.04
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
50.4%
annual
RN turnover
38.1%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.14
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.83
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-17 5 5 0 32 1 32
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-04-24 8 6 3 44 1 44
Total weighted health score
35.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (17)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
16 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-27
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2025-04-17 2025-05-27
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-27
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2025-04-17 2025-05-27
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-04-17 2025-05-27
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-04-24 2024-05-10
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-04-24 2024-05-10
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-04-24 2024-05-10
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. C 2024-04-24 2024-04-30
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2024-04-24 2024-05-13
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2024-04-24 2024-04-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-04-20 2023-05-19
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. E 2023-04-20 2023-05-19
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2023-04-20 2023-05-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-04-20 2023-05-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-03-08 2023-03-31
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2023-03-08 2023-03-31

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
